extraction.one_line_summary: SOUPE Joffrey won from distance after sprint trains disorganized at final corner, launching in final 250m without leadout support.
tactical_patterns: VINGEGAARD took intermediate sprint third for 1-second bonus, reducing gap to EVENEPOEL to 3 seconds—signaling GC aggression early.
Sprint teams including LOTTO GROVES lost organization at final left-hander with 1km remaining, creating chaos leadout vacuum.
SOUPE launched from distance (250m) without leadout when traditional trains collapsed, old-school tactic succeeded against modern trains.
GROUPAMA-FDJ positioned MARTINEZ at front in closing kilometers despite no sprint interest, revealing conservative jersey defense over stage hunting.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: actively contesting intermediate sprint for bonus seconds despite being domestique, showing GC ambition beyond supporting role
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: stopped for mechanical, checking knee after earlier crash, mentally struggling as worst-placed GC favorite after Giro podium peak
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: finished fifth without his usual leadout train intact, only HAYES remaining at final corner versus previous stages' full train
rider_signals.SOUPE Joffrey: positive: 35-year-old career leadout man won from 250m solo after recognizing sprint chaos, biggest career result
rider_signals.MARTINEZ Lenny: neutral: GROUPAMA-FDJ committed full team to front-running jersey defense on flat stage, conservative tactics but executed cleanly
